vitals.name: Dartmoor Legend Ultra Sportive
vitals.location: Exeter Racecourse, Kennford, Exeter, Devon, EX6 7XS, England

adventure.explanation: The 200-mile Dartmoor Legend Ultra traverses iconic Dartmoor climbs like Haytor and passes through Princetown three times, delivering the UK's toughest one-day cycling challenge beyond the Yorkshire Beast.[1][3]

altitude.explanation: Riders tackle high points on Dartmoor National Park's rugged terrain, including the Haytor Hill Climb segment, though maximum altitudes remain below extreme alpine levels.[1][2]

climate.explanation: Starting at 05:00 from Exeter Racecourse in July, riders face variable Devon weather, with one 2025 finisher noting 7-degree chills and dark descents to Exmouth.[1][5]

community.explanation: The event fosters camaraderie through live GPS tracking for dot-watching, a bag drop at the Postbridge feed station visited three times, and post-ride massage.[1][3]

elevation.explanation: The route demands ~19,700 ft (6,000 m) of ascent over 200 miles across Dartmoor, confirmed by a 2025 finisher's 6,066 m total.[1][2][5]

expenses.explanation: Entry costs £90 for the 200-mile ultra, including GPS tracker, finisher medal, event T-shirt, five feed stations, and bike recovery service.[4][3]

experience.explanation: Finishers like Peter from Surrey Cycling Club in 2025 completed 318.5 km with 6,066 m elevation in 15:57, hitting 83 kph on descents after starting at Exeter Racecourse.[5]

field_depth.explanation: Around 100-200 starters typical for ultra sportives, with top riders like 9th-place finisher averaging 23.3 kph, but lacking elite professional depth.[5][6]

length.explanation: The Legend route spans 200 miles (320 km or 318.5 km per 2025 data), far exceeding the 110-mile Extreme and 64-mile Challenge options.[1][2][5]

logistics.explanation: Event HQ at Exeter Racecourse offers free parking, toilets, food, and 4am registration, with five feed stations, chip timing, and broom wagon recovery.[1][3]

race_quality.explanation: Professional features include chip timing, GPS trackers, signed routes, free photos, first aid, mechanical support, and finisher medals within 19 hours.[1][2][3]

technicality.explanation: Dartmoor's rugged roads feature tough climbs like Haytor, multiple Princetown passages with pace checks, and a final steep ascent to Exeter Racecourse.[1][4][7]

value.explanation: For £90, participants receive T-shirt, medal, GPX files, five feed stations with proper food, live tracking, and recovery services on the 200-mile route.[3][4]

cultural_impact.explanation: Growing legend status within British ultra-cycling community.

prestige.explanation: Marketed as tougher than Majorca 312, yet primarily a UK regional sportive without international pro fields or major podium accolades.[1][3]

course_description.character: Brutal figure-eight through Dartmoor National Park featuring iconic granite climbs, coastal roads, and remote moorland traverses. Riders pass Postbridge feedstation three times, Princetown twice, creating rhythm of suffering and recovery across Devon's wild interior.

suffering_zones.label: Haytor Climb
suffering_zones.description: Iconic Dartmoor granite tor ascent - relentless gradients through exposed moorland.

suffering_zones.label: Princetown Triple Passage
suffering_zones.description: Passing through remote prison town three times with pace clock pressure each visit.

suffering_zones.label: Postbridge Bag Drop Loop
suffering_zones.description: Main feedstation visited three times - psychological checkpoint amid accumulating fatigue.

suffering_zones.label: Coastal Rollercoaster
suffering_zones.description: Exmouth to Dawlish undulating seaside roads - deceptive after moorland brutality.

course_description.signature_challenge: 19-hour time limit from 5AM to midnight averaging 10.5mph including all stops - Dartmoor takes no prisoners.

logistics.overview: Exeter Racecourse HQ just off M5/A38 junction. 5AM mass start, 19-hour cutoff to midnight. Bag drop at Postbridge feedstation. GPS tracking enables dot-watching. Free parking, camping available.
logistics.accommodation: Camping available at event village. Exeter hotels 5 miles away. Local B&Bs in Kennford.
logistics.getting_there: M5 southbound exit, A38 to Exeter Racecourse (EX6 7XS). Sharp right turn after Haldon Hill crest.
logistics.parking: Free parking at Exeter Racecourse venue.
logistics.spectator_info: GPS dot-watching online. Spectators welcome at Postbridge feedstation (visited 3x).

climate.overview: Southwest England summer with Dartmoor microclimate. Mild temps but sudden rain, fog, and headwinds common.
climate.altitude_effects: Dartmoor peaks 1400-1700ft - cumulative fatigue more impactful than acute altitude.

terrain.terrain_overview: 100% paved roads through Dartmoor National Park. Mix of narrow moorland lanes, coastal undulation, and village connectors.

history.overview: Part of Moor to Sea Sportive revival. Dartmoor Legend established as UK's toughest one-day sportive, claiming superiority over Yorkshire Beast and Dragon Ride.
history.notable_editions: 2025 new Exeter Racecourse start/finish. 318km/6066m edition completed in 15:57 by 9th place finisher.
history.growth_trajectory: Incorporated into expanded Moor to Sea event family with 4 distances. Steady growth in ultra-cycling popularity.

final_verdict.one_liner: UK's definitive ultra sportive - 200 miles of Dartmoor savagery.
final_verdict.best_for: Endurance masochists seeking supported ultra-distance road suffering with full logistics.
final_verdict.not_ideal_for: Speed-focused racers, gravel purists, or anyone valuing sleep over 19-hour pace clocks.
final_verdict.pro_tip: Train 10.5mph average including stops. Leggings for 5AM coastal chill. Bag drop nutrition for Postbridge laps 2-3.

citations.title: Dartmoor Legend Ultra - Sportiva Events
citations.snippet: The UK's toughest sportive: a 200 mile ultra cycling challenge taking on some of Dartmoor's most iconic climbs.

citations.title: Moor to Sea & Dartmoor Legend - Sportiva Events

citations.title: Dartmoor Legend Ultra 2025 - Surrey Cycling Club
citations.snippet: Distance: 318.5km Elevation: 6066m Elapsed time: 15:57 Position: 9th. Start - Exeter Racecourse 5AM.
